assessment_items.question_content:
Which of the following does not apply to the Ivory Panel with Archangel?



![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c637864276a59ba467367afa111c7e8e.jpeg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The ivory is large compared with other Byzantine ivory panels.

choices.content: The ivory is very small compared with other Byzantine ivory panels.
The architecture recalls the apse of a church.
The ivory panel was originally part of a diptych.
The ivory shows a combination of pagan and Christian traditions.

assessment_items.question_content:
A gold background in Byzantine art represents the earthly realm.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: A gold background in Byzantine art represents heaven.

assessment_items.question_content:
The angel's face and hair in this ivory are reminiscent of the . . .

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e551a3e139e64630431637dd61bda2c0.jpeg)

[[☃ radio 1]]

Which of the following does not apply to this building?

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/79760afe9c09dfb220019a681cb83e78.jpeg)

Photo: Garth T (CC BY 2.0)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Like San Vitale in Ravenna, Hagia Sophia in present-day Istanbul is a centrally planned church, not a basilica.

choices.content: It follows a traditional basilica design.
It is one of the great technological achievements of the Byzantine Empire.
It was commissioned by the Emperor Justinian
The building's most significant feature is its dome.

assessment_items.question_content:
A gold halo signals that a figure is holy or sacred.

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6a62946f25047650db07426d3ec99bd8.jpeg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Haloes (round, golden shapes or light around the head) are common in medieval and Byzantine art and  indicate that the figure is holy or sacred. In this image, a saint is represented with a halo.

assessment_items.question_content:
The setting for the scene we see in this mosaic is . . . 

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/14780977e368585b5c64e36c9f7aa51b.jpeg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Gold backgrounds indicate the space of heaven in Byzantine art. The four rivers emerging from the base of the globe pinpoint paradise as the location.
St. Apollinare was the first Bishop of Ravenna and is shown here in church vestments (clothing).

choices.content: one of the witnesses to Christ's transfiguration.

assessment_items.question_content:
**The Greek Ionic order was used for the column capitals at Hagia Sophia.**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c1511b18300a3df0915c315512dd88a1.jpeg)

Photo: William Allen

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The column capitals at Hagia Sophia show some elements of the Greek orders (such as scrolls that come from the Ionic order) but are mostly new Byzantine inventions.
